content.text: The perimeter of a closed plane figure is the total distance covered along its boundary. For any polygon, it is the sum of the lengths of all its sides.

content.title: Standard Formulas
items: **Triangle**: Sum of the lengths of its three sides

content.title: Example-1
content.question: Akshi wants to put lace around a rectangular tablecloth that is 3 m long and 2 m wide. Find the length of the lace required.
content.solution:
1. Length = 3 m, Breadth = 2 m.
2. Lace required = Perimeter of the rectangle.
3. Perimeter = $2 \times (3 + 2) = 2 \times 5 = 10$ m.
Hence, 10 m of lace is needed.

content.title: Example-2
content.question: Find the distance travelled by Usha if she takes three rounds of a square park of side 75 m.
content.solution:
1. Side of the park = 75 m.
2. Perimeter = $4 \times 75 = 300$ m.
3. Distance for 1 round = 300 m.
4. Distance for 3 rounds = $3 \times 300 = 900$ m.
Hence, she travels 900 m.

content.text: Regular polygons have all sides and all angles equal (e.g., square, equilateral triangle). The perimeter is simply the number of sides multiplied by the side length.

content.title: General Formula
content.text: Perimeter of any regular polygon = $\text{Number of sides} \times \text{Side length}$.

content.title: Example-3
content.question: A string of 36 cm is used to form a regular hexagon. What is the length of each side?
content.solution:
1. Total length (Perimeter) = 36 cm.
2. Number of sides in a hexagon = 6.
3. Each side = $36 \div 6 = 6$ cm.

content.text: The amount of surface region enclosed by a closed figure is called its area. Area is generally measured in square units (sq m, sq cm).

content.title: Example-4
content.question: A floor is 5 m long and 4 m wide. A square carpet of side 3 m is laid on it. Find the area of the floor NOT carpeted.
content.solution:
1. Area of floor = $5 \times 4 = 20$ sq m.
2. Area of carpet = $3 \times 3 = 9$ sq m.
3. Area not carpeted = $20 - 9 = 11$ sq m.

content.title: Example-5
content.question: The area of a rectangular garden 25 m long is 300 sq m. What is its width?

content.text: Area of irregular shapes can be estimated using a square grid. Conventions include counting full squares as 1, ignoring less than half, and counting more than half as 1.

content.title: Tangram Area
procedure: Notice that Shapes A and B have the same area, and Shape D has twice the area of Shape C.

content.text: A rectangle can be split into two identical triangles by drawing a diagonal. The area of each triangle is exactly half the area of the rectangle.

content.title: Example-6
content.question: If a rectangle ABCD has an area of 40 sq cm, what is the area of triangle BAD formed by its diagonal?
content.solution:
Area of the triangle is half of the area of the rectangle.
Area = $40 \div 2 = 20$ sq cm.

content.text: Changing the shape of a figure can change its perimeter even if the area remains the same.

content.title: Example-7
content.question: Using 9 unit squares, what are the smallest and largest possible perimeters?
content.solution:
1. **Smallest**: A $3 \times 3$ square gives perimeter = $4 \times 3 = 12$ units.
2. **Largest**: A $9 \times 1$ rectangle gives perimeter = $2 \times (9 + 1) = 20$ units.

glossary.definition: The total distance covered along the boundary of a closed plane figure.
The amount of surface region enclosed by a closed figure.
A closed figure with all sides and all angles equal.
A triangle with all three sides of equal length.
The standard unit of measurement for area (e.g., square centimeter).

summary_points: Perimeter is the length of the boundary of a shape, while area measures the surface inside it.
Formulas: Rectangle Perimeter = $2(l+b)$, Area = $l \times b$; Square Perimeter = $4s$, Area = $s \times s$.
Regular polygons follow the pattern: Perimeter = $n \times \text{side length}$.
Areas of complex shapes can be estimated using grid paper conventions.
The area of a triangle formed by a rectangle's diagonal is half the area of that rectangle.
Shapes with the same area can have different perimeters depending on their arrangement.
